title: cepa fistulosa
intro: Cepa fistulosa, also known as the horned pasque flower, is a rare, perennial plant species native to the high-altitude regions of the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe and heal wounds. The health benefits of cepa fistulosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote skin and tissue repair, and provide relief from pain and discomfort. Cepa fistulosa can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically to support skin health and overall well-being.
